---
title: "GymIntelligence"
date: "2026-03-10"
categories: [Swift, iOS, Computer Vision, SwiftUI]
---

## Overview

GymIntelligence is an iOS app that uses your phone's camera and Apple's Vision framework to analyze squat form in real time — entirely on-device, no external APIs or cloud processing required. Point your camera at yourself during a set, and the app tracks your joint positions, counts reps, flags form errors, and records session history so you can review your progress over time.

The goal: give every lifter access to the kind of instant, science-based form feedback that was previously only possible with a trained coach watching you live.

## GitHub Source

<div class="d-flex justify-content-center gap-3 my-4">
  <a class="btn btn-outline-dark d-inline-flex align-items-center"
     href="https://github.com/tyhoag/gymintelligence"
     target="_blank" rel="noopener"
     aria-label="Open GitHub source">
    <i class="fa-brands fa-github me-2" aria-hidden="true"></i>
    View source on GitHub
  </a>
</div>

> **Note:** GymIntelligence is a native iOS app and cannot be embedded in a browser. Install via TestFlight or direct Xcode build from the repo above.

## Technical Details

**Framework/Stack:** Swift, SwiftUI, Apple Vision, AVFoundation, SwiftData

**Deployment:** iOS 17+ (TestFlight / direct install)

**Tools Used:**

- Claude Code
- Xcode / Swift Playgrounds

## Product Requirements Document

### Overview

**Product Name**: GymIntelligence
**Platform**: iOS 17+
**Technology**: On-device pose estimation via Apple Vision — no internet connection required during use

An iOS app that provides real-time, science-based squat form analysis using the phone camera, giving lifters instant feedback on joint angles, rep counts, and form warnings without needing a coach.

---

### Problem Statement

Strength training form is one of the most critical — and most neglected — aspects of fitness:

- **Injuries from poor form** are extremely common, especially at the knee and lower back, and are largely preventable with proper technique
- **Coaching is expensive and inaccessible** — most lifters train alone and never receive real-time feedback on their mechanics
- **Video review is manual and slow** — recording yourself and scrubbing through video after a session is time-consuming and requires self-knowledge to interpret
- **Existing apps are passive** — they record video but provide no automated analysis

---

### Solution

GymIntelligence uses on-device pose estimation to analyze squats in real time:

- The camera captures the lifter's body
- Apple Vision's human body pose detection identifies 19 joint landmarks per frame
- A biomechanics engine computes joint angles and compares them to science-based thresholds
- Live form warnings appear on-screen during the set, not after
- Reps are counted automatically based on hip depth and joint angle transitions
- Sessions are stored locally with per-rep form scores for review

---

### Architecture Overview

```
AVFoundation (camera frames)
        │
        ▼
VisionTracker (actor)
  └── VNDetectHumanBodyPoseRequest
        │
        ▼
LiftSessionViewModel (ObservableObject)
  ├── SquatAnalyzer          → rep detection, phase tracking
  └── BiomechanicsEngine     → joint angle calculations, form thresholds
        │
        ▼
SwiftUI Views
  ├── CameraPreviewView      → live camera feed
  ├── SkeletonOverlayView    → joint landmark visualization
  ├── FormWarningBanner      → real-time alert display
  ├── RepCounterView         → live rep count + phase indicator
  └── SessionHistoryView     → past sessions, per-rep scores
```

**System Design Note:** The `VisionTracker` is implemented as a Swift `actor` — a concurrency primitive that serializes access to its state. This is important because Vision processing runs on a background thread, while SwiftUI rendering happens on the main thread. The actor boundary prevents data races without manual locking. This is the same pattern used in Apple's own sample code for real-time vision tasks.

---

### Core Features

#### 1. Real-Time Rep Counter
- Detects squat phases: standing → descent → bottom → ascent → lockout
- Counts reps based on hip depth crossing parallel (thigh ≈ horizontal)
- Displays current phase and rep count live on screen

#### 2. Joint Angle Display
- Computes and displays angles at:
  - **Knee** (lateral view): valgus/varus collapse detection
  - **Hip** (sagittal view): depth and forward lean
  - **Ankle** (sagittal view): dorsiflexion range
- Angles update every frame (~30 fps on iPhone 12+)

#### 3. Form Warnings with Severity Levels

| Warning | Trigger Condition | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| Knee cave | Knee-hip horizontal distance < threshold | High |
| Insufficient depth | Hip crease above knee at bottom | Medium |
| Forward lean | Torso angle > 45° from vertical | Medium |
| Heel rise | Ankle dorsiflexion > ROM limit | Low |
| Asymmetry | Left/right joint angle delta > 10° | Medium |

#### 4. Skeleton Overlay
- 19-point body landmark skeleton drawn over the live camera feed
- Color-coded joints: green (good), yellow (caution), red (error)
- Bones connecting joints rendered as lines scaled to device screen

#### 5. Session History
- Each session stored locally via SwiftData
- Per-rep records: timestamp, joint angles at bottom position, form score (0–100)
- Session summary: total reps, average form score, most common warnings

#### 6. Video Recording
- Records session video alongside analysis
- Video saved to Photos with session metadata

#### 7. Per-Rep Form Scoring
- Score 0–100 computed at the bottom of each rep
- Weighted across: depth (40%), knee tracking (30%), torso angle (20%), symmetry (10%)

---

### Science-Based Form Thresholds

Thresholds are derived from peer-reviewed biomechanics research:

| Parameter | Threshold | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Squat depth (hip crease below knee) | Required for full ROM | Schoenfeld (2010) — *J Strength Cond Res* |
| Knee valgus limit | < 5° medial displacement | Hewett et al. (2005) — *Am J Sports Med* |
| Torso forward lean | < 45° from vertical | Rippetoe & Kilgore — *Starting Strength* |
| Ankle dorsiflexion | ≥ 20° for safe squat mechanics | Backman & Danielson (2011) |
| Knee-toe alignment | Knee tracks over 2nd–3rd toe | NSCA Guidelines |

---

### Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Language | Swift 5.9 |
| UI | SwiftUI |
| Pose Estimation | Apple Vision (`VNDetectHumanBodyPoseRequest`) |
| Camera | AVFoundation (`AVCaptureSession`) |
| Persistence | SwiftData |
| Concurrency | Swift Concurrency (async/await, actors) |
| Deployment | iOS 17+ (TestFlight) |
| IDE | Xcode 15 |

---

### MVP Success Criteria

- **Rep accuracy ≥ 90%** — verified against manual count across 3 test subjects
- **False positive warnings ≤ 2 per set** — form alerts should not fire on correct reps
- **Tested on 3 people** with different body types (to catch landmark tracking variance)
- **Zero crashes** during a 10-rep set at 30 fps
- **Session saves successfully** and displays correct rep count in history

---

### Out of Scope (V1)

- Bench press and deadlift analysis — squat only for MVP
- Cloud sync or account system — all data stays on device
- Audio cues — visual warnings only
- Subscription or monetization model
- Android / cross-platform support

---

### Future Enhancements

- [ ] Bench press and deadlift analysis modules
- [ ] Audio coaching cues during set
- [ ] Apple Watch integration for heart rate overlay
- [ ] AI coaching summary after each session (on-device LLM or API)
- [ ] Export session data as CSV / share to coach
- [ ] Cloud sync with iCloud
- [ ] Side-by-side video comparison (current vs. previous best)
- [ ] Barbell detection and load tracking via camera
- [ ] Social sharing of form scores
